features.description: Base capability. Every tenant has the seeded 10-entry catalog (U6..Masters). Always on across every tier.

Tenant can create custom (non-seeded) age groups beyond the platform default catalog. Off on Small tier (locked to seeded catalog); Medium up to 20 customs; Enterprise unlimited. Backed by the `age_group_custom` entitlement + the `age_group_slot` slot.

Tenant can edit fields on seeded (platform-baseline) age groups: rename, recolour, adjust sort_order + description + gender_category. Bounds + cutoff + sport_key edits still require the more restrictive per-field entitlements. Off on Small tier — Small tenants get the platform baseline verbatim. Medium + Enterprise carry this flag.

Tenant can attach a sport_key to an AgeGroup, defining sport-specific overrides (e.g. football U12 with Jan 1 European federation cutoff). Enterprise-only. Backed by the `age_group_sport_specific` entitlement.
Tenant can scope AgeGroups to a specific Organization (nullable organization_id — non-null = brand-specific). Medium + Enterprise (Small tier has 1 org by default so this is a no-op there).
Tenant can archive a custom age group + restore it within 1095 days. Small tier cannot archive because they cannot create customs; Medium + Enterprise can archive their customs (never seeded rows).
Tenant can call GET /api/v1/age-groups/resolve to preview which age group a DOB + sport_key falls into. All tiers — the resolver is safeguarding-critical for every enrollment flow.
